prompt: If Jim is a politician, then Jim couldn't be bold
hint: Does 'couldn't' mean 'not possible' or 'not necessary'? Think about what's being ruled out.
explanation: This sentence is scope-ambiguous. 'Couldn't be bold' is □∼B (necessarily not bold). The box can take NARROW scope — P ⊃ □∼B, 'if Jim is a politician then it's impossible for him to be bold' (option 1) — or WIDE scope — □(P ⊃ ∼B), 'necessarily, if Jim is a politician he isn't bold' (option 2). English doesn't settle which, so BOTH are defensible. (The reading □P ⊃ ∼B puts necessity on the antecedent and isn't natural.)
